title: 3 REITs I'm Avoiding And 1 REIT I Love For H2 2023
text: The first half of 2023 was marked by an increase in the S&P 500. A handful of stocks drove the gains. I now expect REITs to take over. Here are three I'm avoiding and one I like a lot.

title: Why I'm Overweight Tech-Trifecta
text: For industrial REITs the strong fundamentals (fueled by growth in online sales) bode well for the continuation of excellent tenant demand and strong rent increases. We also remain bullish on data center REITs because demand prospects are improving (e.g., bookings of new leases and rent pricing). For cell towers, we remain positive on the long-term secular growth trends underpinning wireless.

title: REIT Earnings Preview: Here's What We're Watching
text: Real estate earnings season kicks into gear this week, and over the next month, we'll hear results from 175 equity REITs, 40 mortgage REITs, and dozens of housing industry companies. Real estate equities - the sector with perhaps the most to gain from a moderation in inflation and normalization in Fed monetary policy - enter earnings season with wind in their sails. Obscured by macro headwinds, REITs delivered a solid first quarter of earnings results fueled by buoyant rent growth. This report discusses the major high-level themes we'll be watching across second quarter results.

title: After Earnings Results, Markets Love Prologis Stock
text: As earnings season kicks off, a different set of stocks goes under the scrutinizing spotlight of financial markets' judgment. In today's market, there are clear signs of favoritism and preference as investors begin to identify what money "likes" in the economy.

title: Prologis: Continuing To Flex Its Pricing Power
text: Leading industrial-operator, Prologis, reported Q2 results that surpassed expectations. Despite an expected moderation in the rental rate environment, results suggest PLD remains securely fastened in the driver's seat. Though shares have gained over the past year, the stock is still underperforming broader markets.

title: Why Prologis Stock Was Slipping Today
text: The company beat estimates on the top and bottom lines and raised guidance. Valuation concerns and a slowdown in industrial real estate seemed to weigh on the stock.

title: Here's What Key Metrics Tell Us About Prologis (PLD) Q2 Earnings
text: While the top- and bottom-line numbers for Prologis (PLD) give a sense of how the business performed in the quarter ended June 2023, it could be worth looking at how some of its key metrics compare to Wall Street estimates and year-ago values.

title: Prologis' (PLD) Q2 FFO Beats on Solid Rent Growth, '23 View Up
text: Prologis' (PLD) second-quarter 2023 results reflect strong industrial real estate demand, leading to robust leasing activity and solid rent growth. It raises its core FFO per share outlook for 2023.

title: Prologis (PLD) Q2 FFO Surpass Estimates
text: Prologis (PLD) came out with quarterly funds from operations (FFO) of $1.83 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.68 per share. This compares to FFO of $1.11 per share a year ago.

title: Prologis Boosts Outlook on Record Quarterly Earnings
text: The logistics-property giant is building and buying warehouses even as interest rates rise and logistics demand falters.

title: Prologis stock rallies after profit and revenue beats, as net rent change in U.S. jumps 92%
text: Shares of Prologis Inc. PLD, +0.18% surged 2.4% toward a five-month high in premarket trading Tuesday, after the logistics real estate giant reported second-quarter profit and revenue that rose well above expectations and boosted its full-year outlook. Net income doubled to $1.22 billion, or $1.31 a share, from $610 million, or 82 cents a share, in the year-ago period.

title: Prologis Reports Second Quarter 2023 Results
text: Premium Global Portfolio Continues to Drive Record Results SAN FRANCISCO , July 18, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Prologis, Inc. (NYSE: PLD), the global leader in logistics real estate, today reported second quarter results for 2023.  Net earnings per diluted share was $1.31 for the second quarter of 2023 compared with $0.82 for the second quarter of 2022.

text: Digitalization, decarbonization, deglobalization, and the continued rise of alternative investments are major long-term investing megatrends. American Tower, Blackstone, Brookfield Infrastructure, Prologis, and NextEra Energy are focused on different megatrends.

title: Prologis: Deglobalization Could Be The Next Dominant Tailwind For This REIT
text: Since the pandemic, deglobalization has been a topic that has further accelerated and could be the next big market factor. Prologis is a logistics REIT and with a market cap of $117B, it's the world's biggest REIT. Prologis generated significant value for its shareholders since going public, with a total return of 117% in the previous five years alone.

text: The U.S. industrial real estate sector, while exhibiting signs of normalization following the extraordinary demand spike during the pandemic, continues to project healthy fundamentals. title: Mom's All-Weather REIT Portfolio (Part 1)
text: This article was targeted for my mother who is looking to put some dry powder to work so she can take advantage of the opportunities in the REIT sector. The article is the first in a two-part series such that my Mom can use this blueprint for her new REIT portfolio. The objective is to construct a diversified portfolio of 20 REITs that consists of some of the highest-quality REITs that are trading at a wide margin of safety.

title: Why Prologis Can Handle an Industrial REIT Downturn
text: Prologis is by far the largest publicly traded industrial REIT with a global portfolio of well-positioned properties. Being large has advantages, but Prologis also stands out for its financial strength.

title: Prologis: Strong Fundamentals But Not Sufficient To Justify The Valuation
text: Prologis, the largest publicly traded industrial REIT, has consistently outperformed the overall REIT market and the S&P 500 over the past decade. Despite high valuations, PLD's strong position, supported by factors such as significant e-commerce exposure and resilient occupancy rates, makes a short position risky. PLD's underpriced lease agreements and strong balance sheet further bolster its potential for long-term value creation and growth.

title: Buy The REIT - Sell The Real Estate
text: While the market is bearish on REITs, those who buy actual real estate don't share the sentiment. While REITs normally trade in-line with the value of their properties, a gap has opened up. We discuss how to take advantage of this gap.

title: Prologis to Announce Second Quarter 2023 Results July 18
text: SAN FRANCISCO , July 3, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Prologis, Inc. (NYSE: PLD), the global leader in logistics real estate, will host a webcast and conference call with senior management to discuss second quarter results, current market conditions and future outlook on Tuesday, July 18, 2023. With this release, the company is updating the time it will hold its conference call to 9:00 a.m.
